# terrible_vyos_vyos_vyos_user (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `aggregate` (String) The set of username objects to be configured on the remote VyOS device. The list entries can either be the username or a hash of username and properties. This argument is mutually exclusive with the `name` argument.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `configured_password` (String) The password to be configured on the VyOS device. The password needs to be provided in clear and it will be encrypted on the device.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `encrypted_password` (String) The encrypted password of the user account on the remote device. Note that unlike the `configured_password` argument, this argument ignores the `update_password` and updates if the value is different from the one in the device running config.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `full_name` (String) The `full_name` argument provides the full name of the user account to be created on the remote device. This argument accepts any text string value.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `name` (String) The username to be configured on the VyOS device. This argument accepts a string value and is mutually exclusive with the `aggregate` argument.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `public_keys` (String) Public keys for authentiction over SSH.
- `purge` (Boolean) Instructs the module to consider the resource definition absolute. It will remove any previously configured usernames on the device with the exception of the `admin` user (the current defined set of users).
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Configures the state of the username definition as it relates to the device operational configuration. When set to *present*, the username(s) should be configured in the device active configuration and when set to *absent* the username(s) should not be in the device active configuration
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `update_password` (String) Since passwords are encrypted in the device running config, this argument will instruct the module when to change the password.  When set to `always`, the password will always be updated in the device and when set to `on_create` the password will be updated only if the username is created.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `commands` (String) The list of configuration mode commands to send to the device
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
